subtasks.description: Evaluate emergency access request against access control policy for P1 incident. Verify requestor role (senior-engineer), urgency level (P1), resource scope (production db-payments-primary), and determine if policy permits immediate grant, requires approval, or denies access.

plan.cost_rationale: Single subtask required: this is an explicit policy evaluation against the governance/access control registry, matching policy_action scope. Mid-tier assigned because emergency access decisions require multi-signal reasoning: (1) requestor identity and role verification, (2) incident severity classification, (3) resource sensitivity (production payments), (4) policy exception rules for P1 incidents, and (5) audit/compliance implications. This exceeds simple single-rule checks and justifies mid-tier complexity.

output.reason: User u_9981 (senior-engineer) has an active P1 incident ticket INC-20241215-0042, satisfying POL-501's incident ticket requirement. POL-502's dual approval requirement is waived under the declared P1 incident exception. POL-503 permits emergency access but mandates post-access logging and review within 24 hours. All three policies are satisfied under P1 emergency conditions, allowing immediate read_write access with mandatory audit and review constraints.
applied_constraints: Access grant must be logged immediately with incident ticket INC-20241215-0042 as the justification reference.
Access session must be fully audited (all queries and write operations recorded).
Access must be reviewed by a DBA or security officer within 24 hours of grant per POL-503.
Access scope is limited to db-payments-primary for the duration of incident INC-20241215-0042 only.
Access must be automatically revoked upon P1 incident closure or within a maximum session window of 4 hours, whichever comes first.
User u_9981 must acknowledge emergency access terms before session initiation.
Incident commander or on-call manager must be notified of this emergency access grant.
